## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Disk survey (threshold 85%): 228 GB: 71%
Heat: rbk-11-mvp-tactical (₣BO)
Status: racing
Created: 260514


## Paces

### rbrd-tripwire-wire (₢BOAAK) [rough]

## Character

Intricate but mechanical. Established host-docker and image-extract
patterns throughout.

## Goal

Make depot-immutable drift fail loud. Levy inscribes a FROM-scratch
image carrying rbrd.env to GAR; every cloud-submitting command pulls
and exact-match diffs the file before proceeding.

## Locked decisions

- rbrd_check and rbrd_inscribe are public functions in a separate
  bespoke implementation module — not in rbrd_regime.sh (which must
  stay uniform with sibling regimes) and not in rbrd_cli.sh's body.
  rbrd_cli.sh sources the bespoke module and exposes both via dispatch
  with differential furnish. New tabtargets rbw-rdc and rbw-rdi.
- Consuming CLIs source the bespoke module, kindle it, call rbrd_check
  inline before cloud submission. No tabtarget preflight orchestration.
- rbrd_check takes a bearer token parameter; role-agnostic. Each
  consuming CLI passes its role's token. No new IAM grants.
- rbrd_inscribe runs host-side at end of successful levy using Payor's
  OAuth access token. Cloud Build is not involved.
- Pre-push existence guard via docker manifest inspect: present → fatal
  "already inscribed, unmake and relevy"; absent → proceed.
- Exact byte-match diff on rbrd.env contents — comments, whitespace,
  line ordering all participate.
- Image lives under rbi_df namespace; specific tag is a mount-time call.

## Done

- Fresh levy ends with the rbrd.env image present in GAR.
- Re-levy on an already-inscribed depot dies with overwrite-fatal.
- Every cloud-submitting command pulls + diffs before submitting; drift
  produces a diff + recovery guidance + fatal; image absent → fatal.
- RBSDE updated for the inscribe step at end of levy; the spec covering
  the kindle-time check identified and updated.
- Handbook documents drift, missing image, and re-inscribe overwrite
  failure modes with recovery guidance.

### rbnRX-convention-document (₢BOAAL) [rough]

## Character

Design conversation requiring judgment. The convention is already in
use by code (rbnnh_ for nameplate hooks, rbndf_ for depot facts, rbndb_
for depot regime bespoke helpers); this pace decides where its formal
documentation lives and writes it there.

## Goal

Lift the self-contained mint record from rbndb_base.sh into the
canonical project documentation location for the rbn{R}{X}_ convention.

## Locked decisions

- Convention: `rbn{regime-letter}{kind-letter}_` is a regime-affiliated
  namespace that allows minting under a terminal regime prefix. Decode:
  `rb` (project) + `n` (extension marker, sidesteps terminal exclusivity
  on regime prefixes) + regime letter + kind letter. Existing uses to
  enumerate: rbnnh_ (nameplate hook), rbndf_ (depot fact image),
  rbndb_ (depot regime bespoke helpers).

## What to decide at mount

- Canonical doc home — candidates: CLAUDE.md prefix registry section,
  a buk*.md guide, BCG addendum, the acronym-selection minting study
  memo (Memos/memo-20260110-...), or a new dedicated location.
  Decide based on the convention's character: project-level naming
  rule (CLAUDE.md fits), BUK-pattern (buk*.md fits), or BCG-internal
  discipline (BCG fits).
- Cross-references: regardless of canonical home, CLAUDE.md's Project
  Prefix Registry or minting workflow should reference the convention
  so future minters discover it.

## Done

- Convention documented in the chosen location with letter-position
  decoding, existing uses enumerated, guidance for future application.
- rbndb_base.sh header trimmed to point at the canonical location
  rather than restating the convention.
- CLAUDE.md references the convention (at minimum a pointer; full
  prose may live elsewhere depending on the home decision).

# jjezs_paddock ₣BO

## Character

Tactical heat. Opportunistic fixes and measurements that surface during
release qualification without being part of the release-finalize path.
Each pace ships a discrete deliverable.

## Environment

- Canest depot: `cancbhl-d-canest2bhl100011`, with
  `RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=e2-highcpu-32` set. 96-core quota in effect;
  cannot be re-levied at default 2-cpu without forfeiting the quota.
- Mac credentials freshened 2026-05-16 via local payor → governor mantle →
  director/retriever investment. Live identities: `director-bhl` and
  `retriever-bhl` on canest 100011. Cerebro's prior `director-bhl` key
  was revoked by the re-investment.
- Cerebro's older `e2-standard-2` depot is GONE. No `gcloud builds list`
  / `describe` path to any baseline build records exists. Baseline
  analysis is log-only.

## Reference data

- May 14 cerebro clean-gauntlet fO logs (canonical baseline,
  whole-conjure granularity):
  `~/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/../logs-buk/hist-rbw-fO-sh-20260514-2*.txt`
  on cerebro. Pulled to Mac at `/tmp/bench-bo/` (temp dir; refresh via
  scp if missing).
- Gauntlet rollup: `hist-rbw-tP-sh-20260514-203134-1368806-985.txt`.
- macOS-local 11-fixture clean passes: May 6 and May 12 (cross-host
  sanity reference).

## Constraints

- No production-config changes from tactical paces unless explicitly
  scoped. Machine-type rollback (if recommended) lands in a follow-up
  pace, not here.
- 96-core quota stays intact — no depot levy/unmake.

# jjezs_paddock ₣BO

## Character

Tactical heat. Opportunistic fixes and measurements that surface during
release qualification without being part of the release-finalize path.
Each pace ships a discrete deliverable.

## Environment

- Canest depot: `cancbhl-d-canest2bhl100011`, with
  `RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=e2-highcpu-32` set. 96-core quota in effect;
  cannot be re-levied at default 2-cpu without forfeiting the quota.
- Mac credentials freshened 2026-05-16 via local payor → governor mantle →
  director/retriever investment. Live identities: `director-bhl` and
  `retriever-bhl` on canest 100011. Cerebro's prior `director-bhl` key
  was revoked by the re-investment.
- Cerebro's older `e2-standard-2` depot is GONE. No `gcloud builds list`
  / `describe` path to any baseline build records exists. Baseline
  analysis is log-only.

## Reference data

- May 14 cerebro clean-gauntlet fO logs (canonical baseline,
  whole-conjure granularity):
  `~/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/../logs-buk/hist-rbw-fO-sh-20260514-2*.txt`
  on cerebro. Pulled to Mac at `/tmp/bench-bo/` (temp dir; refresh via
  scp if missing).
- Gauntlet rollup: `hist-rbw-tP-sh-20260514-203134-1368806-985.txt`.
- macOS-local 11-fixture clean passes: May 6 and May 12 (cross-host
  sanity reference).

## Constraints

- No production-config changes from tactical paces unless explicitly
  scoped. Machine-type rollback (if recommended) lands in a follow-up
  pace, not here.
- 96-core quota stays intact — no depot levy/unmake.

# jjezs_pace ₢BOAAK

## Character

Intricate but mechanical. Established host-docker and image-extract
patterns throughout.

## Goal

Make depot-immutable drift fail loud. Levy inscribes a FROM-scratch
image carrying rbrd.env to GAR; every cloud-submitting command pulls
and exact-match diffs the file before proceeding.

## Locked decisions

- rbrd_check and rbrd_inscribe are public functions in a separate
  bespoke implementation module — not in rbrd_regime.sh (which must
  stay uniform with sibling regimes) and not in rbrd_cli.sh's body.
  rbrd_cli.sh sources the bespoke module and exposes both via dispatch
  with differential furnish. New tabtargets rbw-rdc and rbw-rdi.
- Consuming CLIs source the bespoke module, kindle it, call rbrd_check
  inline before cloud submission. No tabtarget preflight orchestration.
- rbrd_check takes a bearer token parameter; role-agnostic. Each
  consuming CLI passes its role's token. No new IAM grants.
- rbrd_inscribe runs host-side at end of successful levy using Payor's
  OAuth access token. Cloud Build is not involved.
- Pre-push existence guard via docker manifest inspect: present → fatal
  "already inscribed, unmake and relevy"; absent → proceed.
- Exact byte-match diff on rbrd.env contents — comments, whitespace,
  line ordering all participate.
- Image lives under rbi_df namespace; specific tag is a mount-time call.

## Done

- Fresh levy ends with the rbrd.env image present in GAR.
- Re-levy on an already-inscribed depot dies with overwrite-fatal.
- Every cloud-submitting command pulls + diffs before submitting; drift
  produces a diff + recovery guidance + fatal; image absent → fatal.
- RBSDE updated for the inscribe step at end of levy; the spec covering
  the kindle-time check identified and updated.
- Handbook documents drift, missing image, and re-inscribe overwrite
  failure modes with recovery guidance.

# jjezs_pace ₢BOAAL

## Character

Design conversation requiring judgment. The convention is already in
use by code (rbnnh_ for nameplate hooks, rbndf_ for depot facts, rbndb_
for depot regime bespoke helpers); this pace decides where its formal
documentation lives and writes it there.

## Goal

Lift the self-contained mint record from rbndb_base.sh into the
canonical project documentation location for the rbn{R}{X}_ convention.

## Locked decisions

- Convention: `rbn{regime-letter}{kind-letter}_` is a regime-affiliated
  namespace that allows minting under a terminal regime prefix. Decode:
  `rb` (project) + `n` (extension marker, sidesteps terminal exclusivity
  on regime prefixes) + regime letter + kind letter. Existing uses to
  enumerate: rbnnh_ (nameplate hook), rbndf_ (depot fact image),
  rbndb_ (depot regime bespoke helpers).

## What to decide at mount

- Canonical doc home — candidates: CLAUDE.md prefix registry section,
  a buk*.md guide, BCG addendum, the acronym-selection minting study
  memo (Memos/memo-20260110-...), or a new dedicated location.
  Decide based on the convention's character: project-level naming
  rule (CLAUDE.md fits), BUK-pattern (buk*.md fits), or BCG-internal
  discipline (BCG fits).
- Cross-references: regardless of canonical home, CLAUDE.md's Project
  Prefix Registry or minting workflow should reference the convention
  so future minters discover it.

## Done

- Convention documented in the chosen location with letter-position
  decoding, existing uses enumerated, guidance for future application.
- rbndb_base.sh header trimmed to point at the canonical location
  rather than restating the convention.
- CLAUDE.md references the convention (at minimum a pointer; full
  prose may live elsewhere depending on the home decision).

content.text:
## Mounting ₢BOAAK — rbrd-tripwire-wire

**Pace goal**: Inscribe `rbrd.env` as a FROM-scratch image in GAR at the end of levy, then make every cloud-submitting command pull + exact-byte-diff that image before proceeding — so any post-levy hand-edit of `rbrd.env` dies loudly instead of silently no-op'ing.

**Heat context**: ₣BO rbk-11-mvp-tactical — opportunistic tactical fixes. Canest depot pinned at e2-highcpu-32 (96-core quota, cannot re-levy). Just landed ₢BOAAJ which did the naming split; this pace is the tripwire enforcement the BOAAJ message flagged as "the next pace."

**Silks assessment**: `rbrd-tripwire-wire` fits well — *tripwire* names the drift-detection contract, *wire* names the wiring into consuming CLIs. Keep.

**Full docket**:

### Locked
- `rbrd_check` + `rbrd_inscribe` live in a separate bespoke module (not in `rbrd_regime.sh`, not in `rbrd_cli.sh` body). `rbrd_cli.sh` sources it; new tabtargets `rbw-rdc` and `rbw-rdi`.
- Consuming CLIs source the bespoke module, kindle it, call `rbrd_check` inline. No tabtarget preflight orchestration.
- `rbrd_check` takes a bearer-token param; role-agnostic; no new IAM.
- `rbrd_inscribe` runs host-side at end of successful levy using Payor OAuth. No Cloud Build.
- Pre-push existence guard via `docker manifest inspect`: present → fatal "already inscribed, unmake and relevy"; absent → proceed.
- Exact-byte diff (comments, whitespace, line ordering all participate).
- Image under `rbi_df` namespace; specific tag is a mount-time call.

### Done
- Fresh levy ends with `rbrd.env` image present in GAR.
- Re-levy on already-inscribed depot dies with overwrite-fatal.
- Every cloud-submitting command pulls + diffs before submitting; drift → diff + recovery + fatal; image absent → fatal.
- RBSDE updated for inscribe step; kindle-time check spec identified and updated.
- Handbook documents the three failure modes with recovery guidance.

### Proposed approach (4 bullets)

1. **Discovery sweep** — locate the existing depot-regime bespoke module (per ₢BOAAL paddock prose `rbndb_base.sh` exists from ₢BOAAJ); enumerate cloud-submitting CLIs (conjure / enshrine / inscribe / abjure / vouch / pool-build / etc.) that need `rbrd_check`; identify the `rbi_df` tag convention and which spec covers kindle-time checks.

2. **Mint module + tabtargets** — add `rbrd_check` and `rbrd_inscribe` as public functions in the bespoke module; wire `rbrd_cli.sh` dispatch with differential furnish; enroll `rbw-rdc` / `rbw-rdi` via zipper; regenerate tabtarget context.

3. **Wire producers + consumers** — call `rbrd_inscribe` at end of successful levy (with pre-push existence guard); add `rbrd_check $bearer_token` inline at the cloud-submission point of each consuming CLI.

4. **Specs + handbook** — update RBSDE (inscribe step at levy end) and the kindle-time-check spec; add handbook section covering drift / missing-image / re-inscribe-overwrite failure modes with recovery guidance.

### Execution strategy

**Sequential opus** — "intricate but mechanical" per the character line, but the intricate part is real: module-structure choices for the bespoke shim, exact-diff plumbing, multi-CLI consumer enumeration, spec naming/updates. Files independent enough that step 3 could parallelize across CLIs, but pace state and shared bespoke-module shape make sequential safer.

**Verification gates**:
- After steps 1–2: `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` (enrollment + regime validation)
- After step 3: targeted dry-run of one consuming CLI in mock-token mode; full fast suite
- Final: live levy + re-levy + drift-injection on canest is **not possible** (96-core quota constraint says no re-levy) — verification of the inscribe path will have to be partial host-side; the diff path can be exercised by hand-editing local `rbrd.env` and running a cloud-submitter

That last point matters: the "Re-levy dies with overwrite-fatal" Done criterion cannot be live-tested under the heat's quota constraint. We can test the existence-guard logic in isolation (e.g., manifest-inspect mock), but a real end-to-end fresh-levy verification is out of scope on canest100011.

**Ready to proceed?** Want me to start with the discovery sweep (locate bespoke module, enumerate cloud-submitting CLIs, identify kindle-time-check spec)?
Starting the discovery sweep. I'll dispatch an Explore agent to gather all four pieces in parallel.

input.description: Discovery sweep for rbrd tripwire wire

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the discovery report:

---

## Discovery Report: ₢BOAAK Tripwire Implementation Baseline

### 1. Bespoke Module Location & Conventions

**NO bespoke `rbndb_*.sh` module exists yet.** The docket correctly notes that ₢BOAAJ split bespoke helpers into `rbndb_base.sh` — but that file is not present in `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/`. This is expected per the split design: bespoke module **must be created** as part of ₢BOAAK.

**Current structure after BOAAJ split:**
- `rbrd_regime.sh` (line 1, regime validation/rendering) — 50 lines, validates RBRD variables via enrollment
- `rbrd_cli.sh` (line 1, CLI dispatch) — 50 lines, provides `rbrd_validate` and `rbrd_render` commands

**Nameplate hook naming convention (from rbk-claude-acronyms.md):**
- **RBNNH family**: `rbnnh_compose.yml` and `rbnnh_post_charge.sh` live in `.rbk/{moniker}/`
- `rbnnh_compose.yml` — Compose overlay fragment
- `rbnnh_post_charge.sh` — post-charge hook script
- Spec: `/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSCH-charge_hook.adoc`

### 2. Cloud-Submitting CLI Enumeration

Seven submission sites across the Foundry family (all via `rbgu_http_json "POST" ... ZRBFC_GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL`):

**Foundry Director (rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh):**
- Line 1045: `zrbfd_enshrine_submit()` — enshrine image push
- Line 1283: `rbfd_build()` — direct vessel build (FROM-scratch context)
- Line 1610: `zrbfd_mirror_submit()` — manifest mirror operation

**Foundry Ledger (rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh):**
- Line 148: `zrbfl_inscribe_submit()` — reliquary inscription

**Foundry Verify (rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh):**
- Line 403: `zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit()` — graft metadata attachment
- Line 566: `zrbfv_about_submit()` — about-page build
- Line 802: `zrbfv_vouch_submit()` — vouched-image build

**Payor (rbgp_Payor.sh):**
- Line 1254-1255: `zrbgp_pool_probe_submit()` (called twice, tether/airgap) — per-pool capability probes during `rbgp_depot_levy()`

All seven use `rbgu_http_json "POST"` abstraction. **No Payor direct submission sites outside probe context; depot levy delegates to pool probes.**

### 3. rbi_df Namespace & Tag Convention

**Namespace location: `/Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh` lines 173 & 187**

```bash
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS="rbi_df"
```

**Neighboring `rbi_*` siblings (lines 170-173):**
```bash
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS="rbi_hm"        # Director-authored: conjure/kludge/bind/graft
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES="rbi_rq"      # Tools & reliquary artifacts
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES="rbi_es"        # Enshrined manifest copies
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS="rbi_df"      # Payor-authored depot-scoped OCI artifacts
```

**Purpose comment (lines 175-189):** rbi_df is a "flat sea of files. No subdirs. Each filename names one depot-scoped artifact." Today holds only levy-time capability probes (`probe-tether:probe`, `probe-airgap:probe`). **Stale "future RBRD in rbi_df" comment was refreshed in commit fcd56168** (BOAAJ): now correctly states "RBRD ships as its own image at a separate tag" (not inside rbi_df).

**Tag mount-time call:** Locked docket says "specific tag is a mount-time call" — implies tagging is deferred to the pace that wires the inscribe step, NOT fixed in constants.

### 4. Kindle-Time Check Spec Identification

**Two specs document regime-sourcing kindle mechanics:**

1. **RBSRR (RegimeRepo) — `/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc` line 130-135:**
   - Section `//axhro_kindle {rbkro_kindle}` — "Kindles RBRR fields by enrolling each field with type-specific constraints... Detects unexpected RBRR_ variables. Composes docker environment injection set and locks regime against further modification."
   - Covers repository-wide regime (runtime prefix, vessel dir, secrets dir, build timeouts, docs URL)

2. **RBSRT (RegimeDepot) — `/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc` line 68-73:**
   - Section `//axhro_kindle {rbkro_kindle}` — "Kindles RBRD fields by enrolling each field with type-specific constraints... Detects unexpected RBRD_ variables. Locks regime against further modification."
   - Covers depot-time-immutable regime (CLOUD_PREFIX, DEPOT_MONIKER, GCP_REGION, GCB_MACHINE_TYPE)

**RBSDE (Depot Levy) current end-of-levy structure (lines 233-254):**
- Axhoot_typed_output block: "Confirm Depot Creation" (success banner) + "Update Depot Tracking" (refresh fact files)
- Axhoc_completion block: documents full infrastructure created + proceed-to-governor hint
- **No pre-submit-check step yet** — that insertion point is lines 241-246 (between "Update Depot Tracking" and completion block)

**Which spec gets the RBRD-check doc update:** RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc gains a new axhos_step (or equivalent output block) documenting the inscribe-step that encodes the depot regime into GAR before submission.

---

**Summary of key discoveries:**
- rbndb_base.sh does NOT exist (new module needed for ₢BOAAK)
- Seven cloud-submitting functions identified; probes in rbgp_Payor.sh lines 1254-1255 are the **only Payor submission sites**
- rbi_df namespace confirmed at rbgc_Constants.sh:173 with full context (neighboring families, flat-sea comment, stale-comment refresh at fcd56168)
- RBSDE spec ready for inscribe-step insertion between "Update Depot Tracking" and completion block (lines 241-246)
- Tabtargets rbw-rdr/rbw-rdv exist; docket mentions rbw-rdc/rbw-rdi (typos or deferred to later pace)

1. **Bespoke module location**: The pace docket says `rbrd_check` and `rbrd_inscribe` will live in "a separate bespoke implementation module — not in rbrd_regime.sh and not in rbrd_cli.sh's body." A sibling pace's paddock mentions `rbndb_base.sh` (depot regime bespoke helpers) already exists from the just-landed ₢BOAAJ split. **Confirm whether `rbndb_base.sh` (or any rbndb_*.sh) exists in Tools/rbk/`. Show its header comment and what functions it currently exports. If it does NOT exist, report that.** Also: what's the convention name for nameplate hook variant — `rbnnh_compose.yml` / `rbnnh_post_charge.sh` per acronyms file.

2. **Cloud-submitting CLI enumeration**: List every shell script under `Tools/rbk/` that submits work to Cloud Build (typically `gcloud builds submit`, `gcloud builds triggers run`, or `rbgp_*` payor helpers that submit pool builds). For each, name the function or call-site that does the submission, so I know where `rbrd_check $bearer_token` needs to be inlined. Group by family: foundry (rbf*), governor/payor (rbgp/rbgg), depot (rbgb / depot-inscribe), director (rbfd*), etc.

3. **rbi_df namespace + tag convention**: The locked decision says "Image lives under rbi_df namespace; specific tag is a mount-time call." Find where `rbi_df` is defined (likely `Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh` per the project memory). Show the constant value, neighboring `rbi_*` family members (so I can see the existing pattern), and any comment about `rbi_df` purpose. There's a "stale 'future RBRD in rbi_df' comment block" mentioned in recent commits — find that too.

4. **Kindle-time check spec identification**: Existing regime-check specs follow a pattern. Find specs that document "kindle" — the regime-sourcing operation that loads `.env` into shell variables. Specifically look for any spec at `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS*-*.adoc` that documents the rbrd kindle/enforce flow, or sibling RBSRR (RegimeRepo) kindle-time mechanics. I need to know WHICH spec gets updated to document the new pre-submit check call.

Also confirm: 
- Existence and current structure of `rbrd_cli.sh` (for dispatch wiring) and `rbrd_regime.sh` (to confirm what's there now after BOAAJ split)
- The new tabtargets `rbw-rdc` and `rbw-rdi` mentioned in the docket — confirm they DON'T yet exist in tt/ (they shouldn't, since this pace mints them)
- Where the depot levy "end of successful levy" hook would go — look at `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh` for the levy function (probably `rbgp_depot_levy` or similar)
- RBSDE (Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc) — its current contents end-of-levy section, since this spec needs the inscribe-step addition

Report under 500 words, file:line where useful. Don't propose solutions, just discover.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the discovery report:

---

## Discovery Report: ₢BOAAK Tripwire Implementation Baseline

### 1. Bespoke Module Location & Conventions

**NO bespoke `rbndb_*.sh` module exists yet.** The docket correctly notes that ₢BOAAJ split bespoke helpers into `rbndb_base.sh` — but that file is not present in `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/`. This is expected per the split design: bespoke module **must be created** as part of ₢BOAAK.

**Current structure after BOAAJ split:**
- `rbrd_regime.sh` (line 1, regime validation/rendering) — 50 lines, validates RBRD variables via enrollment
- `rbrd_cli.sh` (line 1, CLI dispatch) — 50 lines, provides `rbrd_validate` and `rbrd_render` commands

**Nameplate hook naming convention (from rbk-claude-acronyms.md):**
- **RBNNH family**: `rbnnh_compose.yml` and `rbnnh_post_charge.sh` live in `.rbk/{moniker}/`
- `rbnnh_compose.yml` — Compose overlay fragment
- `rbnnh_post_charge.sh` — post-charge hook script
- Spec: `/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSCH-charge_hook.adoc`

### 2. Cloud-Submitting CLI Enumeration

Seven submission sites across the Foundry family (all via `rbgu_http_json "POST" ... ZRBFC_GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL`):

**Foundry Director (rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh):**
- Line 1045: `zrbfd_enshrine_submit()` — enshrine image push
- Line 1283: `rbfd_build()` — direct vessel build (FROM-scratch context)
- Line 1610: `zrbfd_mirror_submit()` — manifest mirror operation

**Foundry Ledger (rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh):**
- Line 148: `zrbfl_inscribe_submit()` — reliquary inscription

**Foundry Verify (rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh):**
- Line 403: `zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit()` — graft metadata attachment
- Line 566: `zrbfv_about_submit()` — about-page build
- Line 802: `zrbfv_vouch_submit()` — vouched-image build

**Payor (rbgp_Payor.sh):**
- Line 1254-1255: `zrbgp_pool_probe_submit()` (called twice, tether/airgap) — per-pool capability probes during `rbgp_depot_levy()`

All seven use `rbgu_http_json "POST"` abstraction. **No Payor direct submission sites outside probe context; depot levy delegates to pool probes.**

### 3. rbi_df Namespace & Tag Convention

**Namespace location: `/Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh` lines 173 & 187**

```bash
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS="rbi_df"
```

**Neighboring `rbi_*` siblings (lines 170-173):**
```bash
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS="rbi_hm"        # Director-authored: conjure/kludge/bind/graft
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES="rbi_rq"      # Tools & reliquary artifacts
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES="rbi_es"        # Enshrined manifest copies
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS="rbi_df"      # Payor-authored depot-scoped OCI artifacts
```

**Purpose comment (lines 175-189):** rbi_df is a "flat sea of files. No subdirs. Each filename names one depot-scoped artifact." Today holds only levy-time capability probes (`probe-tether:probe`, `probe-airgap:probe`). **Stale "future RBRD in rbi_df" comment was refreshed in commit fcd56168** (BOAAJ): now correctly states "RBRD ships as its own image at a separate tag" (not inside rbi_df).

**Tag mount-time call:** Locked docket says "specific tag is a mount-time call" — implies tagging is deferred to the pace that wires the inscribe step, NOT fixed in constants.

### 4. Kindle-Time Check Spec Identification

**Two specs document regime-sourcing kindle mechanics:**

1. **RBSRR (RegimeRepo) — `/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc` line 130-135:**
   - Section `//axhro_kindle {rbkro_kindle}` — "Kindles RBRR fields by enrolling each field with type-specific constraints... Detects unexpected RBRR_ variables. Composes docker environment injection set and locks regime against further modification."
   - Covers repository-wide regime (runtime prefix, vessel dir, secrets dir, build timeouts, docs URL)

2. **RBSRT (RegimeDepot) — `/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc` line 68-73:**
   - Section `//axhro_kindle {rbkro_kindle}` — "Kindles RBRD fields by enrolling each field with type-specific constraints... Detects unexpected RBRD_ variables. Locks regime against further modification."
   - Covers depot-time-immutable regime (CLOUD_PREFIX, DEPOT_MONIKER, GCP_REGION, GCB_MACHINE_TYPE)

**RBSDE (Depot Levy) current end-of-levy structure (lines 233-254):**
- Axhoot_typed_output block: "Confirm Depot Creation" (success banner) + "Update Depot Tracking" (refresh fact files)
- Axhoc_completion block: documents full infrastructure created + proceed-to-governor hint
- **No pre-submit-check step yet** — that insertion point is lines 241-246 (between "Update Depot Tracking" and completion block)

**Which spec gets the RBRD-check doc update:** RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc gains a new axhos_step (or equivalent output block) documenting the inscribe-step that encodes the depot regime into GAR before submission.

---

**Summary of key discoveries:**
- rbndb_base.sh does NOT exist (new module needed for ₢BOAAK)
- Seven cloud-submitting functions identified; probes in rbgp_Payor.sh lines 1254-1255 are the **only Payor submission sites**
- rbi_df namespace confirmed at rbgc_Constants.sh:173 with full context (neighboring families, flat-sea comment, stale-comment refresh at fcd56168)
- RBSDE spec ready for inscribe-step insertion between "Update Depot Tracking" and completion block (lines 241-246)
- Tabtargets rbw-rdr/rbw-rdv exist; docket mentions rbw-rdc/rbw-rdi (typos or deferred to later pace)
Important conflict found — let me verify directly before proposing the plan.

The discovery surfaced one mount-time decision the docket explicitly punted on. Let me surface it before locking in the plan.

**The rbi_df conflict**

The docket says: *"Image lives under rbi_df namespace; specific tag is a mount-time call."*

But `rbgc_Constants.sh:175-189` (refreshed in ₢BOAAJ, the just-landed pace) says explicitly:

> *"The depot-time-immutable identity and pool settings (CLOUD_PREFIX, DEPOT_MONIKER, GCP_REGION, GCB_MACHINE_TYPE) live in the RBRD regime rather than in rbi_df — **RBRD ships as its own image at a separate tag** and is consulted at depot kindle time, not as flat-file lookups."*

So the docket's "rbi_df namespace" claim conflicts with the constants-file comment block that BOAAJ deliberately refreshed. One of them is right and the other needs to follow.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBRD CLI - Command line interface for RBRD depot regime operations

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# Command Functions

# Command: validate - enrollment-based validation report
rbrd_validate() {
  buc_doc_brief "Validate RBRD depot regime configuration via enrollment report"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Validating RBRD depot regime file: ${RBBC_rbrd_file}"
  buv_report RBRD "Depot Regime"
  buc_step "RBRD depot regime valid"
}

# Command: render - diagnostic display of all RBRD fields
rbrd_render() {
  buc_doc_brief "Display diagnostic view of RBRD depot regime configuration"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buv_render RBRD "RBRD - Recipe Bottle Regime Depot" "${RBBC_rbrd_file}"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zrbrd_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrd_regime.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrd_file}"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zburd_enforce
  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrd_kindle
  zrbrd_enforce

  zbupr_kindle
}

buc_execute rbrd_ "Recipe Bottle Depot Regime" zrbrd_furnish "$@"

# eof

27	######################################################################

29	
30	zrbrd_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBRD_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrd already kindled"
32	
33	  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
34	  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
35	
36	  # Enroll all RBRD variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering
37	
38	  buv_regime_enroll RBRD
39	
40	  buv_group_enroll "Depot Identity"
41	  buv_string_enroll  RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX         2   11  "Prefix for cloud-visible resource names (depot project, GAR repo, GCS bucket, pool stem)"
42	  buv_string_enroll  RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER        1   26  "Depot moniker — paired with CLOUD_PREFIX to derive depot project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket"
43	
44	  buv_group_enroll "GCP Region"
45	  buv_gname_enroll   RBRD_GCP_REGION           1   32  "GCP region"
46	
47	  buv_group_enroll "Google Cloud Build Pool"
48	  buv_gname_enroll   RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE     3   64  "Machine type for Cloud Build private worker pool (CE format)"
49	
50	  # Guard against unexpected RBRD_ variables not in enrollment
51	  buv_scope_sentinel RBRD RBRD_
52	
53	  # Lock all enrolled RBRD_ variables against mutation
54	  buv_lock RBRD
55	
56	  readonly ZRBRD_KINDLED=1
57	}
58	
59	zrbrd_sentinel() {
60	  test "${ZRBRD_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrd not kindled - call zrbrd_kindle first"
61	}
62	
63	# Enforce all RBRD enrollment validations and custom format checks
64	zrbrd_enforce() {
65	  zrbrd_sentinel
66	
67	  buv_vet RBRD
68	
69	  [[ "${RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ ]] \
70	    || buc_die "Invalid RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER format: ${RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER} (expected lowercase alphanumeric starting with letter; no hyphens)"
71	
72	  [[ "${RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$ ]] \
73	    || buc_die "Invalid RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX format: ${RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX} (expected lowercase starting with letter, ending in hyphen)"
74	
75	  # Joint-length cap: GCP project IDs max 30 chars. RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID
76	  # composes as "${RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER}".
77	  #
78	  # Test fixtures (rbtdrp_pristine, rbtdrk_canonical) compose BURS_TINCTURE
79	  # (1-3 chars) into BOTH the prefix and the moniker before writing rbrd.env
80	  # for parallel-station disjointness on a shared payor manor. The cap below
81	  # applies to whatever is in rbrd.env at validation time — production-
82	  # authored or fixture-written. Fixture bases are short (≤7 chars) so
83	  # tinctured fixture values stay well under the cap; the joint check exists
84	  # to catch operator-authored prefix+moniker pairs that would overflow.
85	  # The validator does not cross-reference BURS_TINCTURE (BURS may not be
86	  # kindled in every RBRD-enforce context); the literal field values are
87	  # the contract.
88	  local -r z_prefix_len=${#RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX}
89	  local -r z_moniker_len=${#RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER}
90	  local -r z_joint_len=$(( z_prefix_len + 2 + z_moniker_len ))
91	  test "${z_joint_len}" -le 30 \
92	    || buc_die "Joint length exceeds 30-char GCP project ID limit: RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX(${z_prefix_len}) + 'd-'(2) + RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER(${z_moniker_len}) = ${z_joint_len}"
93	}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Regime Depot - Validator Module

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBRD_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrd multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBRD_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbrd_*)

zrbrd_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBRD_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrd already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all RBRD variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll RBRD

  buv_group_enroll "Depot Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX         2   11  "Prefix for cloud-visible resource names (depot project, GAR repo, GCS bucket, pool stem)"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER        1   26  "Depot moniker — paired with CLOUD_PREFIX to derive depot project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket"

  buv_group_enroll "GCP Region"
  buv_gname_enroll   RBRD_GCP_REGION           1   32  "GCP region"

  buv_group_enroll "Google Cloud Build Pool"
  buv_gname_enroll   RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE     3   64  "Machine type for Cloud Build private worker pool (CE format)"

  # Guard against unexpected RBRD_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel RBRD RBRD_

  # Lock all enrolled RBRD_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock RBRD

  readonly ZRBRD_KINDLED=1
}

zrbrd_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBRD_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrd not kindled - call zrbrd_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all RBRD enrollment validations and custom format checks
zrbrd_enforce() {
  zrbrd_sentinel

  buv_vet RBRD

  [[ "${RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ ]] \
    || buc_die "Invalid RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER format: ${RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER} (expected lowercase alphanumeric starting with letter; no hyphens)"

  [[ "${RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$ ]] \
    || buc_die "Invalid RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX format: ${RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX} (expected lowercase starting with letter, ending in hyphen)"

  # Joint-length cap: GCP project IDs max 30 chars. RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID
  # composes as "${RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER}".
  #
  # Test fixtures (rbtdrp_pristine, rbtdrk_canonical) compose BURS_TINCTURE
  # (1-3 chars) into BOTH the prefix and the moniker before writing rbrd.env
  # for parallel-station disjointness on a shared payor manor. The cap below
  # applies to whatever is in rbrd.env at validation time — production-
  # authored or fixture-written. Fixture bases are short (≤7 chars) so
  # tinctured fixture values stay well under the cap; the joint check exists
  # to catch operator-authored prefix+moniker pairs that would overflow.
  # The validator does not cross-reference BURS_TINCTURE (BURS may not be
  # kindled in every RBRD-enforce context); the literal field values are
  # the contract.
  local -r z_prefix_len=${#RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX}
  local -r z_moniker_len=${#RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER}
  local -r z_joint_len=$(( z_prefix_len + 2 + z_moniker_len ))
  test "${z_joint_len}" -le 30 \
    || buc_die "Joint length exceeds 30-char GCP project ID limit: RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX(${z_prefix_len}) + 'd-'(2) + RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER(${z_moniker_len}) = ${z_joint_len}"
}

# eof

local -r z_cb_service_agent="service-${z_project_number}@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.${RBGC_SA_EMAIL_DOMAIN}"
  buc_log_args "CB service agent: ${z_cb_service_agent}"

  buc_step 'Enable Cloud Build service agent to impersonate Mason'
  rbgi_add_sa_iam_role "${z_token}" "${z_mason_sa_email}" "${z_cb_service_agent}" "roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator"

  buc_step 'Submit per-pool probe builds (populate quota rows + assert egress posture + GAR write)'

  local -r z_tether_posture_check="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_tether_posture_check.sh"
  local -r z_airgap_posture_check="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_airgap_posture_check.sh"

  zrbgp_write_posture_check tether "${z_tether_posture_check}"
  zrbgp_write_posture_check airgap "${z_airgap_posture_check}"

  zrbgp_pool_probe_submit "${z_token}" "tether" "${z_tether_id}" "${z_mason_sa_email}" "${z_tether_posture_check}"
  zrbgp_pool_probe_submit "${z_token}" "airgap" "${z_airgap_id}" "${z_mason_sa_email}" "${z_airgap_posture_check}"

  buc_step 'Update depot tracking'
  zrbgp_depot_list_update || buc_die "Failed to update depot tracking after creation"

  buc_success 'Depot creation successful'
  buc_info "Next: mantle Governor for this depot:"
  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR}"
}

rbgp_depot_unmake() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  # Folio arrives via BUZ_FOLIO under the param1 channel (rbz_zipper.sh
  # enrolls RBZ_UNMAKE_DEPOT with channel=param1 per BBAA9). buz_exec_lookup
  # extracts $1 into BUZ_FOLIO before exec'ing this function and removes it
  # from $@, so reading $1 here would always be empty.
  local -r z_project_id="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "DANGER: Permanently destroy an entire depot infrastructure"
  buc_doc_param "depot_project_id" "Full GCP project ID of the depot to destroy (matches what rbw-dl displays)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Refusal ordering (cheapest first per BBAA9 docket):
  #   1. Empty-arg refusal (zero traffic)
  #   2. Live-disqualify (zero traffic, local string compare)
  #   3. Authenticate
  #   4. projects.get + state=ACTIVE + displayName discriminator

  if test -z "${z_project_id}"; then
    buc_warn "rbgp_depot_unmake requires a depot project ID argument"
    buc_info "Run the depot list to view candidate depots:"
    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_LIST_DEPOT}"
    buc_die "Depot project ID required as first argument"
  fi

  if test "${z_project_id}" = "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"; then
    buc_warn "Refusing to unmake the live RBRR-selected depot: ${z_project_id}"
    buc_info "Recovery: rename RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER in rbrr.env, or run rbw-MZ to zero regime, then retry."
    buc_die "Live depot cannot be unmade — would orphan local regime state"
  fi

  buc_step 'Safety confirmation required'
  buc_require "DANGER: Permanently destroy depot ${z_project_id} and ALL resources" "${z_project_id}"

  buc_step 'Authenticate as Payor'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor via OAuth"

  buc_step 'Validate target depot'
  local -r z_project_info_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects/${z_project_id}"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_project_info_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_validate"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Validate depot project" "depot_destroy_validate"

  local z_lifecycle_state
  z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_validate" '.state // "UNKNOWN"') || buc_die "Failed to parse project state"

  if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" != "ACTIVE"; then
    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
      buc_die "Project already marked for deletion"
    else
      buc_die "Project state is ${z_lifecycle_state} - can only destroy ACTIVE projects"
    fi
  fi

  # DisplayName discriminator — symmetric with rbgp_depot_list. Refuses any
  # project whose displayName does not start with the RBGC depot anchor,
  # protecting non-depot projects in the Payor's account from accidental
  # destruction once unmake is decoupled from RBRR.
  local z_display_name
  z_display_name=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_validate" '.displayName // ""') || buc_die "Failed to parse displayName"
  local -r z_display_prefix="${RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX} "
  if [[ "${z_display_name}" != "${z_display_prefix}"* ]]; then
    buc_warn "Project displayName does not match depot anchor: ${z_display_name}"
    buc_info "Run the depot list to view candidate depots:"
    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_LIST_DEPOT}"
    buc_die "Refusing to unmake non-depot project: ${z_project_id}"
  fi

  # Derive moniker and pool stem from the validated displayName + project_id.
  # displayName format: "${RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX} <moniker>"
  # project_id   format: "<cloud_prefix>${RBGC_depot_project_infix}<moniker>"
  # pool_stem    format: "<cloud_prefix><moniker>-pool" (mirrors RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM)
  local -r z_moniker="${z_display_name#${z_display_prefix}}"
  local -r z_cloud_prefix="${z_project_id%${RBGC_depot_project_infix}${z_moniker}}"
  local -r z_pool_stem="${z_cloud_prefix}${z_moniker}-pool"

  buc_step 'Clean up governor service accounts (404-tolerant)'
  # No standalone demantle verb — governor SA lifecycle ends here.  Project
  # deletion would clean these up implicitly, but an explicit DELETE pass
  # gives diagnostic visibility and makes the cleanup contract testable.
  local -r z_unmake_sa_url_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts"
  local z_unmake_sa_page_token=""
  local z_unmake_sa_page=1
  local z_unmake_sa_url=""
  local z_unmake_sa_tok_enc=""
  local z_unmake_sa_infix=""
  local z_unmake_sa_count=0
  local z_unmake_sa_index=0
  local z_unmake_sa_email=""
  local z_unmake_gov_delete_infix=""
  local z_governor_sa_count=0
  while :; do
    z_unmake_sa_url="${z_unmake_sa_url_base}"
    if test -n "${z_unmake_sa_page_token}"; then

#!/bin/bash
# shellcheck disable=SC2153  # kindle chain - per BCG
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle GCP Payor - Billing and Destructive Lifecycle Operations
#
# Scope: this file mixes two concerns; entry points are interleaved.
#   OAuth credential flow:
#     zrbgp_refresh_capture         (~76)   refresh-token exchange
#     zrbgp_authenticate_capture    (~122)  load + exchange
#     rbgp_payor_install            (~400)  full install ceremony
#     rbgp_payor_oauth_refresh      (~1229) display refresh procedure
#   Depot lifecycle operations:
#     zrbgp_billing_attach/detach, liens, bucket helpers (~196-395)
#     rbgp_depot_levy / unmake / list (~568-1163)
#     rbgp_governor_mantle          (~1268) Governor SA reset (writes RBRA)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBGP_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgp multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBGP_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbgp_*)

zrbgp_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGP_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgp already kindled"

  buc_log_args "Ensure dependencies are kindled first"
  zrbgc_sentinel
  zrbgo_sentinel
  zrbgu_sentinel
  zrbgi_sentinel

  readonly ZRBGP_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_"
  readonly ZRBGP_EMPTY_JSON="${ZRBGP_PREFIX}empty.json"
  printf '{}' > "${ZRBGP_EMPTY_JSON}"

  readonly ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_scratch.txt"

  # Infix values for HTTP operations
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS="list_liens"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_DELETE_LIEN="delete_lien"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_ATTACH="billing_attach"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_DETACH="billing_detach"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_INFO="project_info"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE="bucket_create"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_API_CHECK="api_checking"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_LIST_SA="gov_list_sa"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_DELETE_SA="gov_delete_sa"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_CREATE_SA="gov_create_sa"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_KEY="gov_key"

  # Pool build await budget — applies to any Cloud Build dispatched against
  # one of the depot's worker pools (probe at levy, posture check at info).
  # 120 polls × 5s = 10-minute ceiling, generous for cold-start worker
  # assignment and image pulls, well under Cloud Build's default
  # queueTtl=3600s. Foundry builds carry user payload and use the
  # ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_* family kindled in rbfc.
  readonly ZRBGP_POOL_BUILD_POLL_CEILING=120
  readonly ZRBGP_POOL_BUILD_POLL_INTERVAL_SEC=5

  # Posture-check per-request ceiling — cloud-side curl --max-time used by
  # the posture assertion script (zrbgp_write_posture_check) when probing

content.subject: Mint rbndb_base.sh with rbrd_check + rbrd_inscribe
content.description: Create Tools/rbk/rbndb_base.sh per the rbn{R}{X}_ convention (depot regime bespoke). Export rbrd_check (takes bearer token, pulls rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire, exact-byte-diffs against local .rbk/rbrd.env, fatals on drift/missing) and rbrd_inscribe (FROM-scratch image carrying rbrd.env, manifest-inspect existence guard, push to rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire using Payor OAuth bearer). Follow BCG patterns from sibling bespoke modules.

content.subject: Wire rbrd_cli.sh dispatch + enroll rbw-rdc / rbw-rdi tabtargets
content.description: Source rbndb_base.sh in rbrd_cli.sh; add check + inscribe to dispatch with differential furnish (check needs bearer, inscribe needs Payor OAuth context). Enroll rbw-rdc.CheckDepotRegime.sh and rbw-rdi.InscribeDepotRegime.sh tabtargets via rbz_zipper.sh. Regenerate tabtarget context.

content.subject: Hook rbrd_inscribe at end of rbgp_depot_levy
content.description: Add rbrd_inscribe call to rbgp_Payor.sh's depot levy function after successful pool probes, before completion banner. Use Payor OAuth access token. Pre-push existence guard via docker manifest inspect: present → fatal "already inscribed, unmake and relevy"; absent → proceed.

content.subject: Inline rbrd_check at 7 cloud-submitting sites
content.description: Add `rbrd_check $bearer_token` before each gcloud builds submit in: rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh (zrbfd_enshrine_submit, rbfd_build, zrbfd_mirror_submit), rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh (zrbfl_inscribe_submit), rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh (zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit, zrbfv_about_submit, zrbfv_vouch_submit). Each CLI sources rbndb_base.sh and kindles it. Each uses its role's bearer token (director or retriever).

content.subject: Soften rbgc_Constants.sh rbi_df comment block
content.description: Lines 175-189 currently describe rbi_df as flat-file-of-assignments and explicitly say RBRD ships "at a separate tag" (outside rbi_df). Update to reflect that rbi_df now also holds the rbrd:tripwire image. Keep the existing probe documentation; add rbrd:tripwire as a second class of artifact.

content.subject: Update RBSDE for inscribe-at-end-of-levy step
content.description: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc gains an inscribe step in the typed-output block between "Update Depot Tracking" and the completion block. Document the FROM-scratch image, the existence guard, the rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire target, and the Payor OAuth bearer source.

content.subject: Update RBSRT for kindle-time check contract
content.description: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc gains documentation for the pre-cloud-submit check: each cloud-submitting CLI sources the bespoke module, kindles it, calls rbrd_check inline. Document the bearer-token-pass contract (role-agnostic). Cross-reference RBSDE for the producing side.

content.subject: Handbook section for three drift failure modes
content.description: Add handbook content covering: (1) drift — local rbrd.env differs from inscribed image, show diff + recovery guidance; (2) image absent — fatal with re-levy guidance; (3) re-inscribe overwrite — fresh levy on already-inscribed depot fails, must unmake and relevy. Place in appropriate rbh0/ file (likely rbho* onboarding or rbhp* payor — to be decided at mount).

content.subject: Verify with fast suite + regenerate tabtarget context
content.description: Run tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh to regenerate tabtarget context after enrolling rbw-rdc / rbw-rdi. Run tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh to verify enrollment + regime validation. Read logs from ../logs-buk/last.txt to confirm.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle GAR Layout - Implementation
#
# Kindle constants for GAR categorical-namespace path construction.
# Callers interpolate the three root constants with hallmark/date/anchor and
# basename literals at the call site — no subshell, no function call overhead.
#
# Shape:
#   "${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/<hallmark>/<basename>:<hallmark>"
#   "${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/<date>/<tool>:<date>"
#   "${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/<anchor>:<anchor>"
#   "${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_DEPOT_FACTS_ROOT}/<filename>:<tag>"

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBGL_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgl multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBGL_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbgl_*)

zrbgl_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGL_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgl already kindled"

  # Category constants come from rbgc.
  zrbgc_sentinel

  # Root segments — category only. Callers append '/<id>/<basename>:<tag>'.
  readonly RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS}"
  readonly RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES}"
  readonly RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES}"
  readonly RBGL_DEPOT_FACTS_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS}"

  readonly ZRBGL_KINDLED=1
}

zrbgl_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBGL_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgl not kindled - call zrbgl_kindle first"
}

# eof

content.content:
670	# in Google's console UI; (2) step 1 confirms the pool exhibits its
671	# stamped egress posture; (3) step 2 confirms the worker can authenticate
672	# and push to rbi_df under the depot's GAR repository. SUCCESS is the
673	# expected terminal; FAILURE means the in-build posture assertion tripped
674	# and the operator should inspect via the console link.
675	# Builder images are Google-hosted only — airgap cannot reach docker.io.
676	# Args: token pool_variant pool_id mason_email posture_check_file
677	zrbgp_pool_probe_submit() {
678	  zrbgp_sentinel
679	
680	  local -r z_token="${1}"
681	  local -r z_pool_variant="${2}"
682	  local -r z_pool_id="${3}"
683	  local -r z_mason_email="${4}"
684	  local -r z_posture_check_file="${5}"
685	
686	  local -r z_region="${RBRD_GCP_REGION}"
687	  local -r z_pool_resource="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${z_region}/workerPools/${z_pool_id}"
688	  local -r z_mason_sa="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_email}"
689	  local -r z_probe_ref="${z_region}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS}/probe-${z_pool_variant}:probe"
690	
691	  # Push script — pool-specific values baked in at host-side, so Cloud Build
692	  # sees a fully-resolved script with no substitutions. Heredocs inside this
693	  # file are cloud-side bash (executed by the build worker); BCG host-side
694	  # heredoc prohibition does not apply to content destined for cloud execution.
695	  local -r z_push_script_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_probe_${z_pool_variant}_push.sh"
696	  printf '%s\n' \
697	    '#!/bin/bash' \
698	    'set -euo pipefail' \
699	    'mkdir -p /workspace/probe' \
700	    'cat > /workspace/probe/probe-marker.txt <<MARKER' \
701	    'RecipeBottle depot levy probe' \
702	    "pool:    ${z_pool_id}" \
703	    "variant: ${z_pool_variant}" \
704	    "depot:   ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" \
705	    'MARKER' \
706	    'cat > /workspace/probe/Dockerfile <<DF' \
707	    'FROM scratch' \
708	    'COPY probe-marker.txt /' \
709	    'DF' \
710	    "docker build -t \"${z_probe_ref}\" /workspace/probe" \
711	    "docker push \"${z_probe_ref}\"" \
712	    "echo \"GAR push OK: ${z_probe_ref}\"" \
713	    > "${z_push_script_file}" \
714	    || buc_die "Failed to write probe push script for ${z_pool_variant}"
715	
716	  local -r z_build_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_probe_${z_pool_variant}_build.json"
717	  jq -n \
718	    --arg     posture_image   "gcr.io/google.com/cloudsdktool/cloud-sdk:alpine" \
719	    --arg     docker_image    "gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker" \
720	    --rawfile posture_check   "${z_posture_check_file}" \
721	    --rawfile push_script     "${z_push_script_file}" \
722	    --arg     pool_resource   "${z_pool_resource}" \
723	    --arg     service_account "${z_mason_sa}" \
724	    '{
725	      steps: [
726	        {
727	          name:   $posture_image,
728	          id:     "assert-egress-posture",
729	          script: $posture_check
730	        },
731	        {
732	          name:   $docker_image,
733	          id:     "probe-gar-push",
734	          script: $push_script
735	        }
736	      ],
737	      serviceAccount: $service_account,
738	      options: {
739	        logging: "CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY",
740	        pool: { name: $pool_resource }
741	      }
742	    }' > "${z_build_file}" \
743	    || buc_die "Failed to compose probe build JSON for ${z_pool_variant}"
744	
745	  zrbgp_pool_build_submit_await \
746	    "${z_token}" "${z_pool_variant}" "${z_pool_id}" \
747	    "${z_build_file}" "depot_probe" "Per-pool probe" "${z_probe_ref}"
748	}
749	
750	# Depot-info per-pool posture check. Composes a 1-step Cloud Build that
751	# runs the posture-check assertion against an existing worker pool and
752	# delegates to zrbgp_pool_build_submit_await. Distinct from the levy's
753	# 2-step probe: no marker push, no GAR write — purely an egress-posture
754	# diagnostic against a pool that has already been levied and probed.
755	# SUCCESS terminal = pool's egress posture matches its stamped config;
756	# FAILURE = drift worth investigating via the console link.
757	# Args: token pool_variant pool_id mason_email posture_check_file
758	zrbgp_pool_posture_submit() {
759	  zrbgp_sentinel
760	
761	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
762	  local -r z_pool_variant="${2:-}"
763	  local -r z_pool_id="${3:-}"
764	  local -r z_mason_email="${4:-}"
765	  local -r z_posture_check_file="${5:-}"
766	
767	  test -n "${z_token}"              || buc_die "zrbgp_pool_posture_submit: token required"
768	  test -n "${z_pool_variant}"       || buc_die "zrbgp_pool_posture_submit: pool_variant required"
769	  test -n "${z_pool_id}"            || buc_die "zrbgp_pool_posture_submit: pool_id required"
770	  test -n "${z_mason_email}"        || buc_die "zrbgp_pool_posture_submit: mason_email required"
771	  test -n "${z_posture_check_file}" || buc_die "zrbgp_pool_posture_submit: posture_check_file required"
772	
773	  local -r z_region="${RBRD_GCP_REGION}"
774	  local -r z_pool_resource="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${z_region}/workerPools/${z_pool_id}"
775	  local -r z_mason_sa="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_email}"
776	
777	  local -r z_build_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_posture_${z_pool_variant}_build.json"
778	  jq -n \
779	    --arg     posture_image   "gcr.io/google.com/cloudsdktool/cloud-sdk:alpine" \
780	    --rawfile posture_check   "${z_posture_check_file}" \

file.content:
# in Google's console UI; (2) step 1 confirms the pool exhibits its
# stamped egress posture; (3) step 2 confirms the worker can authenticate
# and push to rbi_df under the depot's GAR repository. SUCCESS is the
# expected terminal; FAILURE means the in-build posture assertion tripped
# and the operator should inspect via the console link.
# Builder images are Google-hosted only — airgap cannot reach docker.io.
# Args: token pool_variant pool_id mason_email posture_check_file
zrbgp_pool_probe_submit() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1}"
  local -r z_pool_variant="${2}"
  local -r z_pool_id="${3}"
  local -r z_mason_email="${4}"
  local -r z_posture_check_file="${5}"

  local -r z_region="${RBRD_GCP_REGION}"
  local -r z_pool_resource="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${z_region}/workerPools/${z_pool_id}"
  local -r z_mason_sa="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_email}"
  local -r z_probe_ref="${z_region}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS}/probe-${z_pool_variant}:probe"

  # Push script — pool-specific values baked in at host-side, so Cloud Build
  # sees a fully-resolved script with no substitutions. Heredocs inside this
  # file are cloud-side bash (executed by the build worker); BCG host-side
  # heredoc prohibition does not apply to content destined for cloud execution.
  local -r z_push_script_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_probe_${z_pool_variant}_push.sh"
  printf '%s\n' \
    '#!/bin/bash' \
    'set -euo pipefail' \
    'mkdir -p /workspace/probe' \
    'cat > /workspace/probe/probe-marker.txt <<MARKER' \
    'RecipeBottle depot levy probe' \
    "pool:    ${z_pool_id}" \
    "variant: ${z_pool_variant}" \
    "depot:   ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" \
    'MARKER' \
    'cat > /workspace/probe/Dockerfile <<DF' \
    'FROM scratch' \
    'COPY probe-marker.txt /' \
    'DF' \
    "docker build -t \"${z_probe_ref}\" /workspace/probe" \
    "docker push \"${z_probe_ref}\"" \
    "echo \"GAR push OK: ${z_probe_ref}\"" \
    > "${z_push_script_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write probe push script for ${z_pool_variant}"

  local -r z_build_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_probe_${z_pool_variant}_build.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg     posture_image   "gcr.io/google.com/cloudsdktool/cloud-sdk:alpine" \
    --arg     docker_image    "gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker" \
    --rawfile posture_check   "${z_posture_check_file}" \
    --rawfile push_script     "${z_push_script_file}" \
    --arg     pool_resource   "${z_pool_resource}" \
    --arg     service_account "${z_mason_sa}" \
    '{
      steps: [
        {
          name:   $posture_image,
          id:     "assert-egress-posture",
          script: $posture_check
        },
        {
          name:   $docker_image,
          id:     "probe-gar-push",
          script: $push_script
        }
      ],
      serviceAccount: $service_account,
      options: {
        logging: "CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY",
        pool: { name: $pool_resource }
      }
    }' > "${z_build_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to compose probe build JSON for ${z_pool_variant}"

  zrbgp_pool_build_submit_await \
    "${z_token}" "${z_pool_variant}" "${z_pool_id}" \
    "${z_build_file}" "depot_probe" "Per-pool probe" "${z_probe_ref}"
}

# Depot-info per-pool posture check. Composes a 1-step Cloud Build that
# runs the posture-check assertion against an existing worker pool and
# delegates to zrbgp_pool_build_submit_await. Distinct from the levy's
# 2-step probe: no marker push, no GAR write — purely an egress-posture
# diagnostic against a pool that has already been levied and probed.
# SUCCESS terminal = pool's egress posture matches its stamped config;
# FAILURE = drift worth investigating via the console link.
# Args: token pool_variant pool_id mason_email posture_check_file
zrbgp_pool_posture_submit() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  local -r z_pool_variant="${2:-}"
  local -r z_pool_id="${3:-}"
  local -r z_mason_email="${4:-}"
  local -r z_posture_check_file="${5:-}"

  test -n "${z_token}"              || buc_die "zrbgp_pool_posture_submit: token required"
  test -n "${z_pool_variant}"       || buc_die "zrbgp_pool_posture_submit: pool_variant required"
  test -n "${z_pool_id}"            || buc_die "zrbgp_pool_posture_submit: pool_id required"
  test -n "${z_mason_email}"        || buc_die "zrbgp_pool_posture_submit: mason_email required"
  test -n "${z_posture_check_file}" || buc_die "zrbgp_pool_posture_submit: posture_check_file required"

  local -r z_region="${RBRD_GCP_REGION}"
  local -r z_pool_resource="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${z_region}/workerPools/${z_pool_id}"
  local -r z_mason_sa="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_email}"

  local -r z_build_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_posture_${z_pool_variant}_build.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg     posture_image   "gcr.io/google.com/cloudsdktool/cloud-sdk:alpine" \
    --rawfile posture_check   "${z_posture_check_file}" \
    --arg     pool_resource   "${z_pool_resource}" \
    --arg     service_account "${z_mason_sa}" \
    '{
      steps: [
        {
          name:   $posture_image,
          id:     "assert-egress-posture",
          script: $posture_check
        }

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

zrbfd_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided temporary directory, empty at start of command"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_NOW_STAMP        " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided string unique between invocations"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgl_GarLayout.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${RBBC_rbrd_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgu_Utility.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  zbuv_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate BUD environment'
  zburd_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate required tools'
  command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "git not found - required for assuring controlled build context"

  buc_log_args 'Container runtime settings'
  RBG_RUNTIME="${RBG_RUNTIME:-podman}"
  RBG_RUNTIME_ARG="${RBG_RUNTIME_ARG:-}"

  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrd_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbrd_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle modules in dependency order'
  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgl_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbgu_kindle
  zrbfh_kindle

content.subject: Mint rbndb_base.sh with rbrd_check + rbrd_inscribe
content.description: Create Tools/rbk/rbndb_base.sh per the rbn{R}{X}_ convention (depot regime bespoke). Export rbrd_check (takes bearer token, pulls rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire, exact-byte-diffs against local .rbk/rbrd.env, fatals on drift/missing) and rbrd_inscribe (FROM-scratch image carrying rbrd.env, manifest-inspect existence guard, push to rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire using Payor OAuth bearer). Follow BCG patterns from sibling bespoke modules.

content.subject: Wire rbrd_cli.sh dispatch + enroll rbw-rdc / rbw-rdi tabtargets
content.description: Source rbndb_base.sh in rbrd_cli.sh; add check + inscribe to dispatch with differential furnish (check needs bearer, inscribe needs Payor OAuth context). Enroll rbw-rdc.CheckDepotRegime.sh and rbw-rdi.InscribeDepotRegime.sh tabtargets via rbz_zipper.sh. Regenerate tabtarget context.

content.subject: Hook rbrd_inscribe at end of rbgp_depot_levy
content.description: Add rbrd_inscribe call to rbgp_Payor.sh's depot levy function after successful pool probes, before completion banner. Use Payor OAuth access token. Pre-push existence guard via docker manifest inspect: present → fatal "already inscribed, unmake and relevy"; absent → proceed.

content.subject: Inline rbrd_check at 7 cloud-submitting sites
content.description: Add `rbrd_check $bearer_token` before each gcloud builds submit in: rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh (zrbfd_enshrine_submit, rbfd_build, zrbfd_mirror_submit), rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh (zrbfl_inscribe_submit), rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh (zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit, zrbfv_about_submit, zrbfv_vouch_submit). Each CLI sources rbndb_base.sh and kindles it. Each uses its role's bearer token (director or retriever).

content.subject: Soften rbgc_Constants.sh rbi_df comment block
content.description: Lines 175-189 currently describe rbi_df as flat-file-of-assignments and explicitly say RBRD ships "at a separate tag" (outside rbi_df). Update to reflect that rbi_df now also holds the rbrd:tripwire image. Keep the existing probe documentation; add rbrd:tripwire as a second class of artifact.

content.subject: Update RBSDE for inscribe-at-end-of-levy step
content.description: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc gains an inscribe step in the typed-output block between "Update Depot Tracking" and the completion block. Document the FROM-scratch image, the existence guard, the rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire target, and the Payor OAuth bearer source.

content.subject: Update RBSRT for kindle-time check contract
content.description: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc gains documentation for the pre-cloud-submit check: each cloud-submitting CLI sources the bespoke module, kindles it, calls rbrd_check inline. Document the bearer-token-pass contract (role-agnostic). Cross-reference RBSDE for the producing side.

content.subject: Handbook section for three drift failure modes
content.description: Add handbook content covering: (1) drift — local rbrd.env differs from inscribed image, show diff + recovery guidance; (2) image absent — fatal with re-levy guidance; (3) re-inscribe overwrite — fresh levy on already-inscribed depot fails, must unmake and relevy. Place in appropriate rbh0/ file (likely rbho* onboarding or rbhp* payor — to be decided at mount).

content.subject: Verify with fast suite + regenerate tabtarget context
content.description: Run tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh to regenerate tabtarget context after enrolling rbw-rdc / rbw-rdi. Run tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh to verify enrollment + regime validation. Read logs from ../logs-buk/last.txt to confirm.

content.text: Now I have everything I need. Writing rbndb_base.sh.
