content.content: Updated task #6 status

220	.... `steps[1]`: GAR write probe (builder: `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker`) — `FROM scratch` image with a single marker file, pushed to `«REGION»-docker.pkg.dev/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/«GAR_REPOSITORY»/rbi_df/probe-«VARIANT»:probe`
221	... {rbbc_require}
222	submission returns HTTP 200/201 (build accepted). All other codes are fatal.
223	... {rbbc_await}
224	build terminal state via `builds.get` polled every `ZRBGP_BUILD_POLL_INTERVAL` seconds, with a `ZRBGP_BUILD_POLL_CEILING` ceiling (10 minutes total). SUCCESS is the expected terminal state on correct posture; FAILURE means the in-build assertion tripped (egress-posture mismatch) and operators investigate via the console link. Any other terminal state (CANCELLED / TIMEOUT / EXPIRED / INTERNAL_ERROR) indicates Cloud Build infrastructure trouble and is similarly diagnostic, not silently accepted.
225	... {rbbc_show}
226	per-pool probe block carrying the pool ID, GAR artifact reference, build ID + Cloud Build console link, and terminal status.
227	.. NOTE: Builder images are Google-hosted only (`gcr.io/...`). The airgap pool cannot reach `docker.io`; any non-Google builder would hang the build.
228	.. NOTE: Submission itself populates the per-pool concurrent-build quota row in Google's Cloud Build console UI — what the preflight diagnostic calls "after some build activity." A SUCCESS terminal probe confirms three things end-to-end: the pool can dispatch builds, the worker exhibits the egress posture stamped into its network config, and the worker can authenticate to GAR and push to `rbi_df`. Operators bump per-pool quota afterward via the now-populated console row to enable subsequent parallel gauntlet activity.
229	.. NOTE: Submission awaits terminal state inline. The two iterations of the loop run serially because the await blocks the loop body before the next submission; this is load-bearing. An earlier non-awaiting variant caused the late-clearing-probe-collision wedge — the airgap probe queued for ~19 minutes behind subsequent gauntlet dispatches, and when it finally cleared, the next tether conjure (e.g., `ordain_conjure_jupyter`) wedged in `QUEUED` until `queueTtl=3600s` expired the build. See `Memos/memo-20260517-cloudbuild-default-quota-wedge/`. The serialized await also makes the airgap probe's slot acquisition fast in the no-contention case, since no other gauntlet builds are competing during the levy window.
230	.. NOTE: In-build posture-assertion failure (e.g., airgap pool reaching the public internet, tether pool unable to resolve `storage.googleapis.com`) surfaces in the build's terminal status as FAILURE — a hard infra-drift signal. The posture-check script uses `curl` without `-f` so that HTTP 4xx/5xx responses from a reached server still count as "reached"; only honest network failures (DNS, connection refused, timeout) trip the assertion.
231	.. NOTE: The `rbi_df` namespace is a flat sibling of `rbi_hm` / `rbi_rq` / `rbi_es` under the GAR repository. {rbtgo_image_audit} / {rbtgo_image_rekon} enumerators ignore it by design — probe artifacts are operational, not part of the hallmark / reliquary / enshrine image catalogue. Filenames under `rbi_df` form a flat sea (no subdirs); each filename names one depot-scoped OCI artifact.
232	
233	//axhoot_typed_output
234	{rbtgi_depot}

+.. Build a `FROM scratch` image carrying `.rbk/rbrd.env` and push it host-side to `«REGION»-docker.pkg.dev/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/«GAR_REPOSITORY»/rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire`, authenticating to GAR with the Payor OAuth access token already in scope.
+pre-push existence guard via `docker manifest inspect`: image absent → proceed with build + push; image present → fatal (depot already inscribed; unmake and relevy to refresh).

+.. NOTE: The tripwire freezes the depot-time-immutable RBRD regime (CLOUD_PREFIX, DEPOT_MONIKER, GCP_REGION, GCB_MACHINE_TYPE) into GAR. Every cloud-submitting command thereafter pulls and exact-byte-diffs the inscribed copy against local `.rbk/rbrd.env` before submitting (see {rbsrt}), so a post-levy hand-edit fails loud rather than silently diverging from the depot's actual worker-pool quotas, region, and identity. Implemented host-side (Cloud Build is not involved) by `rbrd_inscribe` in `Tools/rbk/rbndb_base.sh`. The tripwire push runs after the depot-tracking refresh; a failure here leaves the depot live but un-inscribed, recoverable via `tt/rbw-rdi.sh` once the cause is resolved.

Successful execution creates depot infrastructure with configured build bucket, container repository, Mason service account, and private worker pool — all derived from `(RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER)`.

content.subject: Mint rbndb_base.sh with rbrd_check + rbrd_inscribe
content.description: Create Tools/rbk/rbndb_base.sh per the rbn{R}{X}_ convention (depot regime bespoke). Export rbrd_check (takes bearer token, pulls rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire, exact-byte-diffs against local .rbk/rbrd.env, fatals on drift/missing) and rbrd_inscribe (FROM-scratch image carrying rbrd.env, manifest-inspect existence guard, push to rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire using Payor OAuth bearer). Follow BCG patterns from sibling bespoke modules.

content.subject: Wire rbrd_cli.sh dispatch + enroll rbw-rdc / rbw-rdi tabtargets
content.description: Source rbndb_base.sh in rbrd_cli.sh; add check + inscribe to dispatch with differential furnish (check needs bearer, inscribe needs Payor OAuth context). Enroll rbw-rdc.CheckDepotRegime.sh and rbw-rdi.InscribeDepotRegime.sh tabtargets via rbz_zipper.sh. Regenerate tabtarget context.

content.subject: Hook rbrd_inscribe at end of rbgp_depot_levy
content.description: Add rbrd_inscribe call to rbgp_Payor.sh's depot levy function after successful pool probes, before completion banner. Use Payor OAuth access token. Pre-push existence guard via docker manifest inspect: present → fatal "already inscribed, unmake and relevy"; absent → proceed.

content.subject: Inline rbrd_check at 7 cloud-submitting sites
content.description: Add `rbrd_check $bearer_token` before each gcloud builds submit in: rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh (zrbfd_enshrine_submit, rbfd_build, zrbfd_mirror_submit), rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh (zrbfl_inscribe_submit), rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh (zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit, zrbfv_about_submit, zrbfv_vouch_submit). Each CLI sources rbndb_base.sh and kindles it. Each uses its role's bearer token (director or retriever).

content.subject: Soften rbgc_Constants.sh rbi_df comment block
content.description: Lines 175-189 currently describe rbi_df as flat-file-of-assignments and explicitly say RBRD ships "at a separate tag" (outside rbi_df). Update to reflect that rbi_df now also holds the rbrd:tripwire image. Keep the existing probe documentation; add rbrd:tripwire as a second class of artifact.

content.subject: Update RBSDE for inscribe-at-end-of-levy step
content.description: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc gains an inscribe step in the typed-output block between "Update Depot Tracking" and the completion block. Document the FROM-scratch image, the existence guard, the rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire target, and the Payor OAuth bearer source.

content.subject: Update RBSRT for kindle-time check contract
content.description: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc gains documentation for the pre-cloud-submit check: each cloud-submitting CLI sources the bespoke module, kindles it, calls rbrd_check inline. Document the bearer-token-pass contract (role-agnostic). Cross-reference RBSDE for the producing side.

content.subject: Handbook section for three drift failure modes
content.description: Add handbook content covering: (1) drift — local rbrd.env differs from inscribed image, show diff + recovery guidance; (2) image absent — fatal with re-levy guidance; (3) re-inscribe overwrite — fresh levy on already-inscribed depot fails, must unmake and relevy. Place in appropriate rbh0/ file (likely rbho* onboarding or rbhp* payor — to be decided at mount).

content.subject: Verify with fast suite + regenerate tabtarget context
content.description: Run tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh to regenerate tabtarget context after enrolling rbw-rdc / rbw-rdi. Run tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh to verify enrollment + regime validation. Read logs from ../logs-buk/last.txt to confirm.

input.new_string:
//axhro_render
{rbkro_render}

Renders the {rbrd_regime} grouped by section with presentation gates per group.
Sections: Depot Identity, GCP Region, Google Cloud Build Pool.

[[rbrd_tripwire]]
{rbrd_tripwire}::
A `FROM scratch` GAR image carrying the verbatim `.rbk/rbrd.env` assignment file, inscribed at `«REGION»-docker.pkg.dev/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/«GAR_REPOSITORY»/rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire`.
The tripwire makes the depot-time-immutable contract enforceable: because every RBDC-derived resource name (project ID, GAR repository, pool stem, bucket) is computed from the {rbrd_regime} at kindle time, a silent post-levy edit of `.rbk/rbrd.env` would point subsequent operations at a different depot identity or worker-pool configuration than the one actually provisioned.
+
The tripwire is _produced_ exactly once, host-side, at the tail of
{rbtgo_depot_levy}
— after the depot-tracking refresh, using the Payor OAuth access token already in scope.
A pre-push existence guard (`docker manifest inspect`) makes inscription idempotent-by-refusal: an already-present tripwire is fatal, since RBRD is frozen for the depot's productive lifetime and refreshing it requires a fresh depot (unmake + relevy).
+
The tripwire is _consumed_ inline by every cloud-submitting command before it dispatches a build: the command pulls the image, extracts `/rbrd.env`, and exact-byte-compares it (comments, whitespace, and line ordering all participate) against the local `.rbk/rbrd.env`.
A mismatch, a missing image, or a registry/auth failure is fatal with recovery guidance — the command refuses to submit cloud work against a drifted or unverifiable depot regime.
The check takes a bearer token parameter and is role-agnostic; each consuming command passes its own role's token (no new IAM grants).
+
The tripwire is depot-scoped operational state, not part of the hallmark / reliquary / enshrine image catalogue; the image enumerators ignore the `rbi_df` namespace by design.
Both operations are implemented host-side (Cloud Build is not involved) by `rbrd_inscribe` and `rbrd_check` in `Tools/rbk/rbndb_base.sh`, exposed for manual use via the `rbw-rdi` (inscribe) and `rbw-rdc` (check) tabtargets.

content.subject: Mint rbndb_base.sh with rbrd_check + rbrd_inscribe
content.description: Create Tools/rbk/rbndb_base.sh per the rbn{R}{X}_ convention (depot regime bespoke). Export rbrd_check (takes bearer token, pulls rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire, exact-byte-diffs against local .rbk/rbrd.env, fatals on drift/missing) and rbrd_inscribe (FROM-scratch image carrying rbrd.env, manifest-inspect existence guard, push to rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire using Payor OAuth bearer). Follow BCG patterns from sibling bespoke modules.

content.subject: Wire rbrd_cli.sh dispatch + enroll rbw-rdc / rbw-rdi tabtargets
content.description: Source rbndb_base.sh in rbrd_cli.sh; add check + inscribe to dispatch with differential furnish (check needs bearer, inscribe needs Payor OAuth context). Enroll rbw-rdc.CheckDepotRegime.sh and rbw-rdi.InscribeDepotRegime.sh tabtargets via rbz_zipper.sh. Regenerate tabtarget context.

content.subject: Hook rbrd_inscribe at end of rbgp_depot_levy
content.description: Add rbrd_inscribe call to rbgp_Payor.sh's depot levy function after successful pool probes, before completion banner. Use Payor OAuth access token. Pre-push existence guard via docker manifest inspect: present → fatal "already inscribed, unmake and relevy"; absent → proceed.

content.subject: Inline rbrd_check at 7 cloud-submitting sites
content.description: Add `rbrd_check $bearer_token` before each gcloud builds submit in: rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh (zrbfd_enshrine_submit, rbfd_build, zrbfd_mirror_submit), rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh (zrbfl_inscribe_submit), rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh (zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit, zrbfv_about_submit, zrbfv_vouch_submit). Each CLI sources rbndb_base.sh and kindles it. Each uses its role's bearer token (director or retriever).

content.subject: Soften rbgc_Constants.sh rbi_df comment block
content.description: Lines 175-189 currently describe rbi_df as flat-file-of-assignments and explicitly say RBRD ships "at a separate tag" (outside rbi_df). Update to reflect that rbi_df now also holds the rbrd:tripwire image. Keep the existing probe documentation; add rbrd:tripwire as a second class of artifact.

content.subject: Update RBSDE for inscribe-at-end-of-levy step
content.description: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc gains an inscribe step in the typed-output block between "Update Depot Tracking" and the completion block. Document the FROM-scratch image, the existence guard, the rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire target, and the Payor OAuth bearer source.

content.subject: Update RBSRT for kindle-time check contract
content.description: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc gains documentation for the pre-cloud-submit check: each cloud-submitting CLI sources the bespoke module, kindles it, calls rbrd_check inline. Document the bearer-token-pass contract (role-agnostic). Cross-reference RBSDE for the producing side.

content.subject: Handbook section for three drift failure modes
content.description: Add handbook content covering: (1) drift — local rbrd.env differs from inscribed image, show diff + recovery guidance; (2) image absent — fatal with re-levy guidance; (3) re-inscribe overwrite — fresh levy on already-inscribed depot fails, must unmake and relevy. Place in appropriate rbh0/ file (likely rbho* onboarding or rbhp* payor — to be decided at mount).

content.subject: Verify with fast suite + regenerate tabtarget context
content.description: Run tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh to regenerate tabtarget context after enrolling rbw-rdc / rbw-rdi. Run tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh to verify enrollment + regime validation. Read logs from ../logs-buk/last.txt to confirm.

--- payor wrapper structure ---
26:rbho_payor_handbook() {
32:  buh_section "Payor — Establish a Manor and Provision the Depot"
33:  buh_e
34:  buh_line "The ${RBYC_PAYOR} establishes a ${RBYC_MANOR} — an administrative seat"
35:  buh_line "holding the billing account, OAuth client, and operator identity."
36:  buh_line "Unlike other roles that use service account keys, the ${RBYC_PAYOR}"
37:  buh_line "authenticates via OAuth — representing the human project owner."
38:  buh_e
39:  buh_line "By the end of this handbook you will have a ${RBYC_MANOR}, a ${RBYC_DEPOT}"
40:  buh_line "funded under it, and a ${RBYC_GOVERNOR} service account ready to administer it."
41:  buh_e
43:  buh_line "This ceremony takes about 15 minutes."
44:  buh_e
46:  buh_step_style "Step " " — "
48:  buh_step1 "Establish the Manor"
49:  buh_e
50:  buh_line "The ${RBYC_MANORS} GCP project hosts the OAuth client and billing"
51:  buh_line "account. It must be created before any infrastructure can be"
52:  buh_line "provisioned."
53:  buh_e
54:  buh_line "Run the guided setup:"
55:  buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_PAYOR_ESTABLISH}"
56:  buh_e
57:  buh_line "This guides you through creating the ${RBYC_MANORS} GCP project,"
58:  buh_line "enabling billing, and configuring the OAuth consent screen. The ${RBYC_MANOR}"
59:  buh_line "identity is recorded in ${RBYC_RBRP}."
60:  buh_e
62:  buh_step1 "Install OAuth credentials"
63:  buh_e
64:  buh_line "Step 1 ended with downloading a JSON client secret file from the"
65:  buh_line "OAuth client you just created. Install it:"
66:  buh_e
67:  buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_PAYOR_INSTALL}" "" " \${HOME}/Downloads/client_secret_*.json"
68:  buh_e
69:  buh_line "This walks you through the OAuth authorization flow and stores"
70:  buh_line "the credential securely."
71:  buh_e
72:  buh_line "If you are refreshing an existing credential that has expired:"
73:  buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_PAYOR_REFRESH}"
74:  buh_e

  buh_line "The ${RBYC_PAYORS} job for this ${RBYC_DEPOT} is done unless billing or"
  buh_line "project-level changes are needed."
  buh_e

  buh_tt  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
  buh_e
}

# eof

input.new_string:
buh_line "${RBYC_PAYOR} creates the Depot:"
  buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_LEVY_DEPOT}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "This enables APIs, creates the Artifact Registry repository and"
  buh_line "Cloud Storage bucket, and configures Cloud Build."
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_PAYOR} can list Depots for verification:"
  buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_LIST_DEPOT}"
  buh_e

  buh_line "At the end of a successful ${RBYC_LEVY}, the depot-immutable"
  buh_line "settings in ${RBYC_RBRD} (cloud prefix, depot moniker, region, and"
  buh_line "Cloud Build machine type) are inscribed into the Depot as a"
  buh_line "tripwire image. Every later cloud build pulls that image and"
  buh_line "byte-compares it against your local ${RBYC_RBRD} file before"
  buh_line "submitting, so an accidental post-levy edit fails loud instead of"
  buh_line "silently pointing builds at the wrong project or worker pool."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Three failure modes you may meet, and how to recover:"
  buh_e
  buh_warn "Drift — a cloud build dies reporting a ${RBYC_RBRD} mismatch."
  buh_line "  Your local ${RBYC_RBRD} no longer matches what the Depot was"
  buh_line "  levied with. Restore the file to its inscribed contents (the"
  buh_line "  failure prints a diff), or if the change is intentional, unmake"
  buh_line "  and re-levy the Depot — ${RBYC_RBRD} is frozen for its lifetime:"
  buh_tt  "    " "${RBZ_UNMAKE_DEPOT}" "" " <depot-project-id>"
  buh_tt  "    " "${RBZ_LEVY_DEPOT}"
  buh_e
  buh_warn "Missing tripwire — a cloud build dies reporting the image is absent."
  buh_line "  The Depot was levied before the tripwire existed, or the image"
  buh_line "  was removed. Inscribe it once against the current ${RBYC_RBRD}"
  buh_line "  (supply a fresh access token):"
  buh_tt  "    " "${RBZ_INSCRIBE_DEPOT}" "" " \$(gcloud auth print-access-token)"
  buh_e
  buh_warn "Re-inscribe refused — re-running levy on an inscribed Depot dies."
  buh_line "  The tripwire is already present and is never overwritten in"
  buh_line "  place. To refresh it, unmake the Depot and levy fresh:"
  buh_tt  "    " "${RBZ_UNMAKE_DEPOT}" "" " <depot-project-id>"
  buh_tt  "    " "${RBZ_LEVY_DEPOT}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "To verify alignment at any time without submitting a build:"
  buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_CHECK_DEPOT}" "" " \$(gcloud auth print-access-token)"
  buh_e

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Handbook Onboarding - Payor Handbook (establish Manor and Depot)

set -euo pipefail

test -z "${ZRBHOPW_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBHOPW_SOURCED=1

rbho_payor_handbook() {
  zrbho_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Payor — establish a Manor and provision the Depot"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buh_section "Payor — Establish a Manor and Provision the Depot"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_PAYOR} establishes a ${RBYC_MANOR} — an administrative seat"
  buh_line "holding the billing account, OAuth client, and operator identity."
  buh_line "Unlike other roles that use service account keys, the ${RBYC_PAYOR}"
  buh_line "authenticates via OAuth — representing the human project owner."
  buh_e
  buh_line "By the end of this handbook you will have a ${RBYC_MANOR}, a ${RBYC_DEPOT}"
  buh_line "funded under it, and a ${RBYC_GOVERNOR} service account ready to administer it."
  buh_e

  buh_line "This ceremony takes about 15 minutes."
  buh_e

  buh_step_style "Step " " — "

  buh_step1 "Establish the Manor"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_MANORS} GCP project hosts the OAuth client and billing"
  buh_line "account. It must be created before any infrastructure can be"
  buh_line "provisioned."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Run the guided setup:"
  buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_PAYOR_ESTABLISH}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "This guides you through creating the ${RBYC_MANORS} GCP project,"
  buh_line "enabling billing, and configuring the OAuth consent screen. The ${RBYC_MANOR}"
  buh_line "identity is recorded in ${RBYC_RBRP}."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "Install OAuth credentials"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Step 1 ended with downloading a JSON client secret file from the"
  buh_line "OAuth client you just created. Install it:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_PAYOR_INSTALL}" "" " \${HOME}/Downloads/client_secret_*.json"
  buh_e
  buh_line "This walks you through the OAuth authorization flow and stores"
  buh_line "the credential securely."
  buh_e
  buh_line "If you are refreshing an existing credential that has expired:"
  buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_PAYOR_REFRESH}"
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "Provision the Depot"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_DEPOT} is the facility where container images are built and"
  buh_line "stored — a GCP project with a container repository, storage bucket,"
  buh_line "and build infrastructure, funded under the ${RBYC_MANORS} billing account."
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_GOVERNOR} administers the ${RBYC_DEPOT} — creating"
  buh_line "${RBYC_RETRIEVER} and ${RBYC_DIRECTOR} accounts for those who build and"
  buh_line "retrieve container images."
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_PAYOR} creates the Depot:"
  buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_LEVY_DEPOT}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "This enables APIs, creates the Artifact Registry repository and"
  buh_line "Cloud Storage bucket, and configures Cloud Build."
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_PAYOR} can list Depots for verification:"
  buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_LIST_DEPOT}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_PAYOR} creates the Governor service account:"
  buh_tt  "  " "${RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The mantle command writes the key file to its output directory"
  buh_line "and prints two cp commands — one for production install, one"
  buh_line "for assay (test) mode."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Hand the resulting key file to the person who will administer"
  buh_line "this ${RBYC_DEPOT}. After this handoff, the ${RBYC_GOVERNOR} can create"
  buh_line "${RBYC_RETRIEVER} and ${RBYC_DIRECTOR} accounts independently."
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_PAYORS} job for this ${RBYC_DEPOT} is done unless billing or"
  buh_line "project-level changes are needed."
  buh_e

  buh_tt  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
  buh_e
}

# eof

lines: buh_line "${RBYC_PAYOR} can list Depots for verification:"
+  buh_line "At the end of a successful ${RBYC_LEVY}, the depot-immutable"
+  buh_line "settings in ${RBYC_RBRD} (cloud prefix, depot moniker, region, and"
+  buh_line "Cloud Build machine type) are inscribed into the Depot as a"
+  buh_line "tripwire image. Every later cloud build pulls that image and"
+  buh_line "byte-compares it against your local ${RBYC_RBRD} file before"

+  buh_line "Three failure modes you may meet, and how to recover:"

content.text: Let me verify `buh_warn`'s signature and that the payor handbook still renders.

input.description: Check buh_warn signature

content.subject: Mint rbndb_base.sh with rbrd_check + rbrd_inscribe
content.description: Create Tools/rbk/rbndb_base.sh per the rbn{R}{X}_ convention (depot regime bespoke). Export rbrd_check (takes bearer token, pulls rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire, exact-byte-diffs against local .rbk/rbrd.env, fatals on drift/missing) and rbrd_inscribe (FROM-scratch image carrying rbrd.env, manifest-inspect existence guard, push to rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire using Payor OAuth bearer). Follow BCG patterns from sibling bespoke modules.

content.subject: Wire rbrd_cli.sh dispatch + enroll rbw-rdc / rbw-rdi tabtargets
content.description: Source rbndb_base.sh in rbrd_cli.sh; add check + inscribe to dispatch with differential furnish (check needs bearer, inscribe needs Payor OAuth context). Enroll rbw-rdc.CheckDepotRegime.sh and rbw-rdi.InscribeDepotRegime.sh tabtargets via rbz_zipper.sh. Regenerate tabtarget context.

content.subject: Hook rbrd_inscribe at end of rbgp_depot_levy
content.description: Add rbrd_inscribe call to rbgp_Payor.sh's depot levy function after successful pool probes, before completion banner. Use Payor OAuth access token. Pre-push existence guard via docker manifest inspect: present → fatal "already inscribed, unmake and relevy"; absent → proceed.

content.subject: Inline rbrd_check at 7 cloud-submitting sites
content.description: Add `rbrd_check $bearer_token` before each gcloud builds submit in: rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh (zrbfd_enshrine_submit, rbfd_build, zrbfd_mirror_submit), rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh (zrbfl_inscribe_submit), rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh (zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit, zrbfv_about_submit, zrbfv_vouch_submit). Each CLI sources rbndb_base.sh and kindles it. Each uses its role's bearer token (director or retriever).

content.subject: Soften rbgc_Constants.sh rbi_df comment block
content.description: Lines 175-189 currently describe rbi_df as flat-file-of-assignments and explicitly say RBRD ships "at a separate tag" (outside rbi_df). Update to reflect that rbi_df now also holds the rbrd:tripwire image. Keep the existing probe documentation; add rbrd:tripwire as a second class of artifact.

content.subject: Update RBSDE for inscribe-at-end-of-levy step
content.description: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc gains an inscribe step in the typed-output block between "Update Depot Tracking" and the completion block. Document the FROM-scratch image, the existence guard, the rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire target, and the Payor OAuth bearer source.

content.subject: Update RBSRT for kindle-time check contract
content.description: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc gains documentation for the pre-cloud-submit check: each cloud-submitting CLI sources the bespoke module, kindles it, calls rbrd_check inline. Document the bearer-token-pass contract (role-agnostic). Cross-reference RBSDE for the producing side.

content.subject: Handbook section for three drift failure modes
content.description: Add handbook content covering: (1) drift — local rbrd.env differs from inscribed image, show diff + recovery guidance; (2) image absent — fatal with re-levy guidance; (3) re-inscribe overwrite — fresh levy on already-inscribed depot fails, must unmake and relevy. Place in appropriate rbh0/ file (likely rbho* onboarding or rbhp* payor — to be decided at mount).

content.subject: Verify with fast suite + regenerate tabtarget context
content.description: Run tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh to regenerate tabtarget context after enrolling rbw-rdc / rbw-rdi. Run tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh to verify enrollment + regime validation. Read logs from ../logs-buk/last.txt to confirm.
